### [Moderation Roles & Privileges](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/moderation/roles-and-privileges)

> Comprehensive guide to user roles, moderation privileges, and administrative controls in social.plus Console for effective community management.

social.plus provides a comprehensive role-based moderation system that enables effective community management through hierarchical permissions, automated tools, and granular control over user interactions across your platform.

Moderation capabilities are determined by user roles, ranging from basic users to global administrators with full platform control.

## Role Hierarchy Overview

    Member, Moderator, and Super-Moderator roles with escalating privileges
    Global Admin and Super-Admin roles for platform administration
    Create custom roles with tailored permissions for specific needs
    Granular control over user actions and moderation capabilities

## User Roles

    ### Standard User Roles

        **Basic User Role:**
        - No moderation privileges
        - Standard content creation and interaction
        - Subject to all moderation rules
        - Can report inappropriate content

        **Capabilities:**
        - Create posts and comments
        - Join communities and channels
        - React to content
        - Send direct messages
        - Update profile information

        **Community Moderation Role:**
        - General moderation privileges
        - Can moderate assigned communities/channels
        - Subject to moderation from Super-Moderators and Admins
        - Enhanced reporting and review capabilities

        **Additional Capabilities:**
        - Delete inappropriate content
        - Mute and unmute users
        - Ban users from channels/communities
        - Review reported content
        - Pin and feature content

        **Enhanced Moderation Role:**
        - All community moderator privileges
        - Exempt from moderation by regular moderators
        - Cross-community moderation capabilities
        - Advanced moderation tools access

        **Enhanced Capabilities:**
        - Global content moderation
        - Cross-community user management
        - Advanced analytics access
        - Escalation handling
        - Policy enforcement

    ### Privilege Comparison

    | Capability | Member | Community Moderator | Global Moderator |
    |------------|---------|-----------|-----------------|
    | Create Content | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Join Communities | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Delete Others' Content | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Mute/Unmute Users | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Ban Users | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
    | Cross-Community Moderation | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
    | Exempt from Moderation | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
    | Advanced Analytics | ❌ | Limited | ✅ |

    ### Moderation Scope

        - Moderate across all communities
        - Access to global user management
        - Platform-wide policy enforcement
        - Cross-community analytics
        - Limited to specific communities
        - Community-specific user management
        - Local policy enforcement
        - Community analytics only

## Admin Roles

    ### Administrative Roles

        **Platform Administrator:**
        - Full moderation privileges across platform
        - User role assignment capabilities
        - Exempt from all moderation actions
        - System configuration access

        **Exclusive Capabilities:**
        - Assign roles to other users
        - Create and manage admin accounts
        - Configure platform-wide settings
        - Access to all analytics and reports
        - Global ban/unban capabilities

        Global Admin role is reserved for administrators only and cannot be assigned to regular users.

        **System Administrator:**
        - Highest level of access
        - Reserved for critical system operations
        - Cannot generate Admin API tokens
        - Used for system-level configurations

        **System Operations:**
        - Push notification certificate management
        - Core system configuration
        - Infrastructure-level settings
        - Emergency system access

        Super-Admin access is automatically granted when accessing console from the social.plus Portal. Create separate admin accounts for regular administrative tasks.

    ### Creating Admin Accounts

        Navigate to Console → **Admin Users** → **Manage Admin Users**
        Click **Create New Admin** and provide credentials
        Configure role permissions and access levels
        Test admin account functionality and access

    ### Admin Best Practices

        **Security Measures:**
        - Use strong, unique passwords
        - Enable two-factor authentication
        - Regular password rotation
        - Monitor admin activity logs
        - Limit admin account creation

        **Access Control:**
        - Principle of least privilege
        - Regular access reviews
        - Separate accounts for different purposes
        - Audit trail maintenance

        **API Token Usage:**
        - Use dedicated admin accounts for API access
        - Never use Super-Admin for API token generation
        - Implement token rotation policies
        - Revoke tokens immediately if compromised

## Role Management

    ### User Role Assignment

        Go to **Community** → **Communities** and select target community
        Click the **Manage Members** tab to view community members
        Choose the user whose role you want to change
        Click **Change user role** button and select new role
        Review selection and click **Confirm** to apply changes

    ### Role Change Considerations

        **Member to Moderator:**
        - Verify user's community engagement
        - Assess understanding of community guidelines
        - Consider user's history and behavior
        - Provide moderation training and resources

        **Moderator to Super-Moderator:**
        - Evaluate moderation performance
        - Assess cross-community needs
        - Verify advanced understanding of policies
        - Consider platform-wide responsibilities

        **Role Reduction:**
        - Document reasons for demotion
        - Communicate changes to affected user
        - Review moderation history
        - Implement gradual privilege reduction
        - Monitor post-demotion behavior

        Communities must maintain at least one moderator. The last remaining moderator cannot be demoted to member status.

    ### Creating Custom Roles

        Navigate to **Users** → **Roles** in the console
        Click **Create a new role** button
        Provide role name and select custom permissions
        Review settings and click **Create** to finalize

    ### Permission Categories

        **Content Permissions:**
        - Create, edit, delete posts
        - Moderate comments and replies
        - Pin and feature content
        - Manage content categories
        - Handle reported content

```json
{
  "contentPermissions": {
    "createPost": true,
    "editPost": true,
    "deletePost": true,
    "moderateComments": true,
    "pinContent": true,
    "featureContent": false
  }
}
```

        **User Permissions:**
        - View user profiles and activity
        - Mute and unmute users
        - Ban and unban users
        - Assign community roles
        - Access user social history

```json
{
  "userPermissions": {
    "viewUserProfiles": true,
    "muteUsers": true,
    "banUsers": true,
    "assignRoles": false,
    "accessUserHistory": true
  }
}
```

        **Community Permissions:**
        - Create and edit communities
        - Manage community settings
        - Control community membership
        - Configure community categories
        - Access community analytics

```json
{
  "communityPermissions": {
    "createCommunity": false,
    "editCommunity": true,
    "manageMembership": true,
    "configureCategories": true,
    "accessAnalytics": true
  }
}
```

## Moderation Tools

    ### Automated Moderation

        **Automated Content Filtering:**
        - Block specific words and phrases
        - Support for exact and partial matching
        - Automatic muting for violations
        - Custom violation responses

        **Moderated Content Types:**
        - Chat messages
        - Posts and comments
        - Poll questions and answers
        - User-generated text content

        **Non-Moderated Content:**
        - Community names
        - Channel names
        - User display names
        - File names
        - System-generated content

```json
{
  "blocklist": {
    "expressions": ["inappropriate_word", "spam_phrase"],
    "matchType": "partial",
    "autoMute": true,
    "muteDuration": 300000,
    "customResponse": "Your message violates community guidelines."
  }
}
```

        **Link Control System:**
        - Default link blocking enabled
        - Whitelist approved domains
        - Pattern-based URL filtering
        - Real-time link validation

        **Configuration Options:**
        - Enable/disable link restrictions
        - Add trusted domains
        - Wildcard domain support
        - Protocol-specific rules

```json
{
  "allowlist": {
    "enabled": true,
    "domains": [
      "example.com",
      "*.trusted-site.org",
      "https://secure.domain.net"
    ],
    "bypassRoles": ["moderator", "admin"]
  }
}
```

    ### User Moderation

        **Platform-Wide Actions:**
        - Remove user from entire platform
        - Disconnect from all channels and communities
        - Delete all chat messages
        - Preserve social content (posts, comments)
        - Prevent re-authentication until unbanned

        **Global Ban Process:**
        1. Navigate to **Users** in console
        2. Select target user
        3. Click ban user icon in **Actions**
        4. Confirm action in popup

        Global ban takes up to 30 seconds to disconnect users. Ban does not emit channel events or update Live Collections.

        **Global Unban Process:**
        1. Go to **Users** in console
        2. Select banned user
        3. Click unban user icon in **Actions**
        4. Confirm unban action

        **Channel-Specific Moderation:**
        - Ban users from specific channels only
        - Soft delete channel messages
        - Real-time ban notifications
        - Immediate LiveCollection updates

        **Channel Ban Process:**
        1. Navigate to **Channels** in console
        2. Select target channel
        3. Click **Members** button
        4. Select user to ban
        5. Click ban icon and confirm

        **Channel Unban Process:**
        1. Go to **Channels** in console
        2. Select relevant channel
        3. Access **Members** section
        4. Select banned user
        5. Click unban icon and confirm

        **Temporary Restrictions:**
        - Prevent message sending
        - Allow message observation
        - Channel-level application
        - Indefinite duration until unmuted

        **Mute Capabilities:**
        - Available to moderators and above
        - Immediate effect implementation
        - Real-time status updates
        - Manual unmute required

## Platform Integration

    ### UI Framework Integration

        **Full Moderation Support:**
        - Network-level moderation
        - Community-level moderation
        - Enhanced moderator menus
        - Real-time permission updates
        **Community-Level Support:**
        - Community moderation only
        - Basic moderator interface
        - Limited cross-community features
        - Standard web interactions


    ### Moderation APIs

        **Role Assignment Endpoints:**
        - `POST /api/v4/communities/{communityId}/users/roles` - Assign community role
        - `PUT /api/v3/users/{userId}/roles` - Update user role
        - `GET /api/v3/users/{userId}/roles` - Get user roles
        - `DELETE /api/v4/communities/{communityId}/users/{userId}/roles` - Remove role

        **Example Role Assignment:**
```bash
curl -X POST "https://api.amity.co/api/v4/communities/123/users/roles" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ADMIN_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "userId": "user456",
    "role": "community-moderator"
  }'
```

        **User Moderation Endpoints:**
        - `POST /api/v3/users/{userId}/ban` - Ban user globally or from channel
        - `DELETE /api/v3/users/{userId}/ban` - Unban user
        - `POST /api/v3/users/{userId}/mute` - Mute user in channel
        - `DELETE /api/v3/users/{userId}/mute` - Unmute user

        **Content Moderation Endpoints:**
        - `DELETE /api/v3/posts/{postId}` - Delete post
        - `DELETE /api/v3/comments/{commentId}` - Delete comment
        - `POST /api/v3/posts/{postId}/flag` - Flag content for review
        - `POST /api/v3/posts/{postId}/approve` - Approve flagged content

## Best Practices

    **Effective Role Management:**
    - **Gradual Progression**: Promote users through role hierarchy gradually
    - **Performance-Based**: Base promotions on demonstrated moderation skills
    - **Community Fit**: Consider user's understanding of community culture
    - **Training**: Provide moderation training before role assignment
    - **Regular Review**: Periodically assess role effectiveness

    **Selection Criteria:**
    - Active community participation
    - Positive user interactions
    - Understanding of platform guidelines
    - Demonstrated leadership qualities
    - Availability for moderation duties

    **Uniform Policy Enforcement:**
    - **Clear Guidelines**: Establish comprehensive moderation guidelines
    - **Decision Documentation**: Record moderation actions and reasoning
    - **Team Communication**: Coordinate between moderators
    - **Policy Updates**: Keep moderation policies current
    - **Training Programs**: Regular moderator training sessions

    **Escalation Procedures:**
    - Define clear escalation paths
    - Set response time expectations
    - Establish appeal processes
    - Document complex decisions
    - Review controversial actions

    **Transparent Moderation:**
    - **Clear Communication**: Explain moderation actions to users
    - **Fair Application**: Apply rules consistently across all users
    - **Appeal Process**: Provide mechanisms for users to appeal decisions
    - **Feedback Collection**: Gather community feedback on moderation
    - **Policy Transparency**: Make community guidelines easily accessible

    **Trust Indicators:**
    - Consistent rule enforcement
    - Responsive to community concerns
    - Clear communication about policy changes
    - Fair handling of disputes
    - Regular community engagement

## Troubleshooting

    **Role Assignment Problems:**
    - **Permission Errors**: Verify admin privileges for role changes
    - **Role Conflicts**: Check for conflicting role assignments
    - **Community Minimums**: Ensure communities maintain minimum moderator count
    - **API Failures**: Verify token permissions and API endpoint access

    **Solutions:**
    - Use Global Admin accounts for role management
    - Resolve role conflicts before new assignments
    - Promote additional moderators before demoting existing ones
    - Check API token scopes and expiration

    **Common Problems:**
    - **Ban Not Effective**: Check user connection status and ban scope
    - **Content Still Visible**: Verify soft deletion vs. hard deletion
    - **Permission Denied**: Confirm moderator privileges and scope
    - **API Timeouts**: Retry operations and check system status

    **Troubleshooting Steps:**
    1. Verify user role and permissions
    2. Check action scope (global vs. channel)
    3. Confirm API token validity
    4. Review system logs for errors
    5. Contact support for persistent issues

---

Effective moderation requires balancing automated tools with human judgment. Regular training and clear guidelines ensure consistent, fair community management.

---

### [Edit User Content with Change History](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/moderation/edit-content-history)

> Modify user-generated content with full transparency and accountability through comprehensive edit tracking and change history management

Empower your moderation team to maintain content quality and compliance by enabling direct editing of user-generated posts and comments through the Console. All modifications are tracked in a comprehensive change history, ensuring transparency, accountability, and audit compliance.

**Key Benefit**: Moderators can correct content violations and enforce community standards without deleting entire posts, while maintaining full transparency through automated change tracking.

    Modify text, media, and metadata for posts and comments
    Track all edits with timestamps, actors, and reasons
    Control who can edit content with role-based access
    Full audit trail for regulatory and policy enforcement
    Remove sensitive information while preserving content
    Users see edit indicators and moderators see full history

## Overview

The Edit User Content feature enables authorized moderators to:

1. **Modify Content**: Edit text, media, and metadata for user posts and comments
2. **Add Context**: Optionally include reasons for edits to improve transparency
3. **Track Changes**: Automatically maintain a comprehensive change history
4. **Enforce Standards**: Remove violations while preserving valuable content
5. **Maintain Compliance**: Ensure all modifications meet community guidelines

## Key Capabilities

    **Post Editing:**
    - **Text Posts**: Edit title and content
    - **Image Posts**: Edit title, content, add/remove/reorder images, modify alt text
    - **Video Posts**: Edit title, content, add/remove/reorder videos, modify alt text
    - **File Posts**: Edit title, content, add/remove/reorder files, modify alt text
    - **Clip Posts**: Edit caption only

    **Comment Editing:**
    - Edit text content
    - Support for both comments and replies
    - Preserve threading and context

    **Restrictions:**
    - ❌ Cannot edit poll posts
    - ❌ Cannot edit stories
    - ❌ Cannot edit livestream posts
    - ❌ Cannot edit scheduled posts
    - ❌ Cannot edit deleted posts

    **What's Tracked:**
    - Original version with creator details
    - All edited versions with modifications
    - Edit timestamps and dates
    - Editor identity (moderator/admin)
    - Optional edit reasons
    - Media additions and removals
    - Deleted child posts

    **History Display:**
    - Latest to oldest chronological order
    - Up to 10 most recent edit versions
    - Original version always visible
    - Clear visual indicators for changes
    - Media deletion status (when feasible)

    **New Permission: "Edit posts and comments"**
    - Separate from "Manage posts and comments"
    - Available to Admin role by default
    - Granular control over editing capabilities

    **Access Requirements:**
    - Must have explicit edit permissions
    - Available in Console/Portal only
    - Subject to role hierarchy
    - Audit logged for all actions

    **Content Validation:**
    - Block word detection during editing
    - Character limit enforcement
    - Media format validation
    - Metadata structure checks

    **Error States:**
    - Block word violation alerts
    - API connection failures
    - Network connectivity issues
    - Concurrent edit conflicts

## Getting Started

    Ensure your admin account has the "Edit posts and comments" permission. Navigate to **Admin users** → **Roles & permissions** to verify role permissions.

    Find the content you want to edit through:
    - **Posts & Comments** page
    - **User History** page

    Click the **⋮** → **Edit** button on the content you want to modify. The edit interface will display based on content type.

    Edit the content according to your moderation needs:
    - Update text content
    - Add, remove, or reorder media
    - Modify alt text for accessibility
    - Adjust metadata as needed

    Provide a clear reason for the edit to improve transparency and maintain audit compliance. This appears in the change history.

    Review your modifications and click **Save**. The system will validate content and update both the Console and user-facing views.

    Confirm that:
    - Content displays correctly
    - "View change history" indicator appears
    - Change history is updated
    - Edit appears in moderation activity report

## Permission Management

### Setting Up Edit Permissions

    ### Admin Role Permissions

    The "Edit posts and comments" permission is automatically available to the **Admin** role and can be viewed in the Admin Roles section.

    **To View Permissions:**

        Go to **Settings** → **Admin Roles** in the Console
        Click on the **Admin** role to view details
        Locate "Edit posts and comments" in the permissions list

    The "Edit posts and comments" permission is distinct from "Manage posts and comments" and provides specific editing capabilities without full content management access.

    ### Permission Assignment Guidelines

        **Separation of Duties:**
        - Create distinct roles for different moderation tasks
        - Separate editing from deletion and banning powers
        - Use "Edit posts and comments" for content refinement
        - Use "Manage posts and comments" for full content lifecycle

        **Principle of Least Privilege:**
        - Grant only necessary permissions for each role
        - Review permissions regularly
        - Audit role usage patterns
        - Remove unused or excessive permissions

        **Recommended Role Hierarchy:**

        1. **Content Editors**: Edit permissions only
        2. **Community Moderators**: Edit + basic moderation
        3. **Senior Moderators**: Edit + full moderation
        4. **Administrators**: All permissions

        **Scaling Considerations:**
        - Small teams: Fewer, broader roles
        - Large teams: More specialized roles
        - Global teams: Regional role assignments
        - Contractor teams: Limited permission sets

        **Audit Requirements:**
        - Log all permission changes
        - Review edit actions monthly
        - Track editor performance metrics
        - Maintain permission documentation

        **Regulatory Considerations:**
        - Document edit reason requirements
        - Establish edit approval workflows
        - Define retention policies for change history
        - Ensure GDPR/CCPA compliance

## Editing Content

### Post Editing Workflows

    ### Editing Text Posts

    Text posts support comprehensive editing of title and content fields.

    **Available Edits:**
    - Title modification
    - Content text updates
    - Metadata adjustments

    **Edit Process:**

        Click **Edit** on the text post you want to modify
        Update the title and/or content fields as needed
        Optionally add an edit reason (e.g., "Removed profanity", "Corrected misinformation")
        System automatically checks for block words and policy violations
        Click **Save** to publish the edited content

    **Example Use Cases:**

        Remove profanity or offensive language while preserving the post's intent and value
        Fix factual errors, correct misinformation, or update outdated information
        Remove personal information, contact details, or sensitive data
        Modify content to meet legal requirements or community guidelines

    ### Editing Image, Video & File Posts

    Media posts support extensive editing including content, media management, and accessibility features.

    **Available Edits:**
    - Title and content text
    - Add new media files
    - Remove existing media
    - Reorder media items
    - Update alt text for accessibility

    **Media Management:**

        Click **Edit** on any image, video, or file post
        Modify title and description as needed
        - **Add**: Upload new images, videos, or files
        - **Remove**: Delete inappropriate or unwanted media
        - **Reorder**: Drag and drop to change media sequence
        Edit alt text descriptions for screen readers and accessibility compliance
        Document why media was modified (e.g., "Removed copyrighted image", "Added accessibility text")
        Review all modifications and save to publish updates

    **Media Deletion**: When removing media files, the change history will show a preview as deleted media. But won't preserve the actual media file itself.

    **Example Scenarios:**

        **Situation**: User uploaded copyrighted images without permission

        **Action**:
        - Remove copyrighted images
        - Keep other original media
        - Add edit reason: "Removed copyrighted content"
        - Preserve post for community visibility

        **Situation**: Post contains mix of appropriate and inappropriate images

        **Action**:
        - Remove offensive images only
        - Maintain appropriate content
        - Update post description if needed
        - Document removal reason

        **Situation**: Media lacks proper alt text descriptions

        **Action**:
        - Add descriptive alt text for all media
        - Improve accessibility compliance
        - Enhance user experience for screen readers
        - Note: "Added accessibility descriptions"

    ### Editing Clip Posts

    Clip posts have limited editing capabilities focused on captions.

    **Clip Posts:**
    - Edit caption text only
    - Cannot modify video content
    - Can add edit reasons

    **Edit Process:**

        Navigate to the clip post
        Open the simplified editor for caption modification
        Modify the caption (clips)
        Provide edit reason for transparency
        Publish the updated caption

    **Design Rationale**: Clip posts have limited editing to preserve the integrity of time-sensitive or real-time content. Only metadata can be modified.

### Comment Editing

    ### Editing Comments and Replies

    Comments and replies support text-only editing to maintain threading integrity.

    **Available Edits:**
    - Text content modification
    - Support for comments and replies
    - Preserve threading context

    **Edit Steps:**

        Find the comment in:
        - Posts & Comments page
        - User History page
        - Moderation Feed
        Click **Edit** on the comment or reply
        Update the comment text as needed
        Document why the comment was edited
        System checks for block words and violations
        Publish the edited comment

    ### Comment Editing Guidelines

        **Edit Comments When:**
        - Minor policy violations (profanity, spam elements)
        - Valuable discussion with problematic elements
        - Educational opportunity (show corrected version)
        - Context preservation is important

        **Delete Comments When:**
        - Severe policy violations (harassment, threats)
        - Entirely inappropriate content
        - Spam or bot-generated content
        - Legal removal requirements

        **Threading Integrity:**
        - Preserve conversation flow
        - Don't alter meaning drastically
        - Consider reply context
        - Maintain chronological sense

        **Communication:**
        - Use clear edit reasons
        - Be transparent about changes
        - Document policy violations
        - Provide user guidance when possible

        **Editorial Principles:**
        - Minimal necessary changes
        - Preserve user voice when possible
        - Fix violations without rewriting
        - Maintain factual accuracy

        **Consistency:**
        - Apply same standards across all comments
        - Use standardized edit reasons
        - Follow escalation procedures
        - Document decision rationale

## Change History

### Understanding Change History

    ### Viewing Change History

    Every edited post or comment maintains a comprehensive change history accessible to moderators.

    **Access Points:**

        View history from main content pages
        Access from user profile sections
        Historical data in moderation reports

    **History Contents:**

        - Complete original content
        - Creator name and details
        - Creation date and timestamp
        - Original media attachments

        - Updated text content
        - Media additions/removals
        - Edit date and timestamp
        - Editor (moderator/admin) identity
        - Edit reason (if provided)

        - Latest edit at top
        - Up to 10 recent edits shown
        - Original version always visible
        - Clear version numbering

    **History Limit**: The system displays the 10 most recent edit versions plus the original post. If content has been edited more than 10 times, only the latest 10 edits are shown.

    ### What's Tracked in History

        **Complete Original Content:**
        - Full text content (title, body, captions)
        - All original media attachments
        - Creator user information
        - Creation timestamp (date and time)
        - Original metadata and tags
        - Initial engagement metrics

        **Preservation:**
        - Always retained in history
        - Never overwritten or deleted
        - Serves as baseline for comparison
        - Maintains attribution integrity

        **For Each Edit:**
        - **Text Changes**: Updated title, content, captions
        - **Media Changes**: Added/removed/reordered files
        - **Timestamp**: Exact date and time of edit
        - **Editor Identity**: Moderator/admin who made changes
        - **Edit Reason**: Optional explanation (if provided)
        - **Deleted Child Posts**: References to removed nested content

        **Version Information:**
        - Sequential version numbering
        - Clear change indicators
        - Before/after comparisons
        - Visual diff highlighting (when applicable)

        **Concurrent Edits:**
        - All versions preserved if multiple moderators edit simultaneously
        - Latest saved version displays to users
        - All versions visible in history
        - Timestamp determines display order

        **Deleted Content:**
        - Change history removed when post is deleted
        - Edits not preserved after deletion
        - Consider archiving before deletion if needed

        **Media Deletion:**
        - System attempts to show deleted media preview
        - Fallback to deletion indicator if preview unavailable
        - Media filename and type preserved
        - Reason for removal documented

    ### Practical Applications

        **Regulatory Requirements:**
        - Complete edit trail for legal compliance
        - Timestamped moderator actions
        - Documented reasons for changes
        - Exportable for legal discovery

        **Internal Audits:**
        - Review moderator decision quality
        - Identify patterns in policy enforcement
        - Assess edit necessity and appropriateness
        - Train new moderators with examples

        **Moderator Performance:**
        - Review editing consistency
        - Assess reason quality
        - Identify training needs
        - Recognize excellence

        **Policy Effectiveness:**
        - Track common violations
        - Identify emerging issues
        - Refine moderation guidelines
        - Measure edit impact

        **Transparency:**
        - Show users what changed
        - Explain policy enforcement
        - Build trust through visibility
        - Demonstrate fairness

        **Education:**
        - Help users understand violations
        - Provide improvement guidance
        - Show correct alternatives
        - Encourage compliance

## Validation & Error Handling

### Content Validation

    ### Block Word Detection

    The system automatically validates edited content against your configured block word list before saving.

    **How It Works:**

        System scans edited content for configured block words
        Clear error message indicates which words triggered the block
        Moderator modifies content to remove or replace blocked terms
        System re-checks content upon next save attempt
        Content saves once all validation checks pass

    **Important**: Block word validation applies to edited content even though moderators are editing. This ensures policy consistency and prevents accidental policy violations.

    ### API & Connectivity Issues

        **Error Scenarios:**
        - Server timeout during save operation
        - API endpoint unavailable
        - Authentication token expired
        - Rate limiting exceeded

        **User Experience:**
        - Clear error message displayed
        - Edit content preserved in editor
        - Retry option provided
        - Unsaved changes warning

        **Resolution Steps:**
        1. Check internet connectivity
        2. Refresh authentication if needed
        3. Retry save operation
        4. Contact support if persistent

        **Connection Issues:**
        - Internet connection lost
        - Network timeout
        - DNS resolution failures
        - Firewall blocking

        **Handling:**
        - Detect connection status
        - Display connectivity warning
        - Queue changes locally (if possible)
        - Auto-retry when connection restored
        - Prevent data loss

        **Conflict Scenarios:**
        - Multiple moderators editing same content
        - Content changed by another moderator during edit
        - Version mismatch on save

        **Resolution:**
        - All versions preserved in history
        - Latest save operation wins
        - Both edits visible in change history
        - Conflict notification to moderators
        - Option to review other changes

        **Content Issues:**
        - Character limit exceeded
        - Invalid media format
        - Corrupted file upload
        - Metadata structure errors

        **Error Messages:**
        - Specific validation failure reason
        - Character count if limit exceeded
        - Supported formats for media
        - Suggestions for correction
        - Links to help documentation

    ### Error Prevention Strategies

        **Before Saving Edits:**
        - Review content for block words manually
        - Check character limits
        - Verify media file formats
        - Ensure edit reason is clear
        - Preview changes if possible

        **Workflow Tips:**
        - Save frequently for long edits
        - Use draft mode if available
        - Keep original in separate window for reference
        - Document complex changes incrementally

        **Stable Environment:**
        - Use reliable internet connection
        - Avoid public Wi-Fi for important edits
        - Keep session active during edits
        - Monitor connection status

        **Backup Strategies:**
        - Copy content before major edits
        - Save complex edits in external document first
        - Screenshot original for reference
        - Note key changes in separate log

        **Prevent Conflicts:**
        - Communicate with team about active edits
        - Use assignment systems to claim content
        - Check if others are editing same content
        - Coordinate on complex multi-moderator cases

        **Conflict Resolution:**
        - Review both versions in history
        - Discuss with other moderator if needed
        - Make additional edit if necessary
        - Document coordination in edit reasons

## Use Cases

    **Scenario**: User posts valuable information with minor profanity

    **Solution**:
    - Edit to remove offensive language
    - Preserve valuable content and discussion
    - Add reason: "Removed inappropriate language"
    - Maintain community value while enforcing standards

    **Scenario**: User accidentally shares personal contact information

    **Solution**:
    - Remove phone numbers and email addresses
    - Keep rest of post intact
    - Add reason: "Removed personal information for privacy"
    - Protect user while preserving content

    **Scenario**: Post contains factually incorrect information

    **Solution**:
    - Correct false statements
    - Add source references if appropriate
    - Add reason: "Corrected factual inaccuracies"
    - Consider adding moderator note to original

    **Scenario**: Post includes copyrighted images without permission

    **Solution**:
    - Remove copyrighted media only
    - Maintain user's text content
    - Add reason: "Removed copyrighted images"
    - Preserve post value while ensuring compliance

    **Scenario**: Legitimate post with embedded spam links

    **Solution**:
    - Remove spam URLs and promotional content
    - Keep relevant discussion points
    - Add reason: "Removed spam links"
    - Maintain conversation flow

    **Scenario**: Media posts lacking proper alt text

    **Solution**:
    - Add descriptive alt text to all media
    - Improve accessibility compliance
    - Add reason: "Added accessibility descriptions"
    - Enhance user experience for all users

    **Scenario**: Content requires modification for regulatory compliance

    **Solution**:
    - Edit to meet legal requirements
    - Document compliance reason clearly
    - Add reason: "Modified for legal compliance"
    - Maintain audit trail for records

    **Scenario**: Content violates specific community guidelines

    **Solution**:
    - Edit to align with community standards
    - Preserve user intent where possible
    - Add reason: "Edited for policy compliance"
    - Use as educational opportunity

## Best Practices

    **When to Edit:**
    - Content has value but contains violations
    - Minor policy infractions
    - Fixable privacy issues
    - Misinformation that can be corrected
    - Accessibility improvements needed
    - First-time violations by good-faith users

    **When to Delete:**
    - Severe policy violations (harassment, threats)
    - Entirely inappropriate content
    - Spam or bot-generated posts
    - Repeated violations after warnings
    - Legal removal requirements
    - Content with no salvageable value

    **Decision Matrix:**

    | Severity | Value | History | Action |
    |----------|-------|---------|--------|
    | Minor | High | First | ✅ Edit |
    | Minor | Low | First | ⚠️ Edit or Warn |
    | Moderate | High | First | ✅ Edit + Warn |
    | Moderate | Low | Repeat | ❌ Delete |
    | Severe | Any | Any | ❌ Delete + Ban |

    **Clear and Specific:**
    - "Removed profanity (specific words)"
    - "Corrected factual error about X"
    - "Removed personal contact information"
    - "Deleted copyrighted image"

    **Avoid Vague Reasons:**
    - ❌ "Policy violation"
    - ❌ "Inappropriate"
    - ❌ "Fixed"
    - ❌ "Updated"

    **Professional Language:**
    - Neutral and objective tone
    - Focus on policy, not judgment
    - Provide context when helpful
    - Use standard terminology

    **Reason Templates:**
```
Privacy: "Removed personal information (phone/email)"
Profanity: "Removed offensive language"
Misinformation: "Corrected factual inaccuracy about [topic]"
Copyright: "Removed copyrighted content"
Spam: "Removed promotional links/spam"
Accessibility: "Added alt text for accessibility"
Legal: "Modified for legal compliance with [law/regulation]"
```

    **Transparency Principles:**
    - Always show edit indicators
    - Provide clear edit reasons
    - Make history accessible
    - Be consistent in enforcement

    **Communication:**
    - Consider notifying users of significant edits
    - Explain policy rationale when appropriate
    - Offer guidance for future compliance
    - Be responsive to questions about edits

    **Fairness:**
    - Apply same standards to all users
    - Document decision rationale
    - Allow for context and intent
    - Provide path to compliance

    **Optimization Strategies:**
    - Use templates for common edit scenarios
    - Develop standardized edit reasons
    - Create team style guide
    - Share best edit examples
    - Automate where appropriate

    **Time Management:**
    - Prioritize high-impact content
    - Batch similar edits when possible
    - Set time limits for complex decisions
    - Escalate edge cases
    - Focus on prevention through education

    **Team Coordination:**
    - Regular calibration sessions
    - Shared edit reason library
    - Peer review for complex cases
    - Clear escalation procedures
    - Knowledge sharing culture

    **Self-Review Checklist:**
    - [ ] Edit preserves user intent where possible
    - [ ] All policy violations addressed
    - [ ] Edit reason is clear and specific
    - [ ] No introduction of new errors
    - [ ] Accessibility maintained or improved
    - [ ] Change history will make sense to reviewers

    **Peer Review Indicators:**
    - Highly visible content
    - Complex policy questions
    - Significant content changes
    - Sensitive topics
    - Legal implications
    - Unusual circumstances

    **Continuous Improvement:**
    - Review edit outcomes
    - Track user responses
    - Analyze effectiveness metrics
    - Update guidelines based on learnings
    - Share successes and challenges

## Troubleshooting

    **Possible Causes:**
    - Insufficient permissions
    - Content type not editable (polls, stories, scheduled posts)
    - Content has been deleted
    - Browser cache issues

    **Solutions:**
    1. Verify you have "Edit posts and comments" permission
    2. Check Admin Roles section for your permissions
    3. Confirm content type supports editing
    4. Clear browser cache and reload
    5. Contact administrator to verify role assignment

    **Cause**: Content contains words from the block word list

    **Solutions:**
    1. Review error message for specific blocked words
    2. Remove or replace blocked terms
    3. Rephrase content to avoid triggers
    4. Verify block word list is current and appropriate
    5. Contact administrator if block word seems incorrect

    **Possible Causes:**
    - Cache delay
    - Save operation failed
    - Browser synchronization issue
    - CDN propagation delay

    **Solutions:**
    1. Refresh the page
    2. Check if edit appears in change history
    3. Verify save confirmation was received
    4. Clear browser cache
    5. Wait 1-2 minutes for propagation
    6. Try from incognito/private browser window
    7. Contact support if persistent

    **Situation**: Another moderator edited the same content

    **What Happens:**
    - Both edits preserved in history
    - Latest save wins for display
    - Both moderators notified

    **Resolution:**
    1. Review both versions in change history
    2. Communicate with other moderator
    3. Make additional edit if needed to reconcile
    4. Document coordination in edit reason

    **Possible Causes:**
    - Content was deleted (history not retained)
    - More than 10 edits (only latest 10 shown)
    - System error during history recording

    **Solutions:**
    1. Verify content hasn't been deleted
    2. Check that you're viewing the correct content
    3. Remember only 10 most recent edits are shown
    4. Review moderation activity report for full log
    5. Contact support if history appears corrupted

    **Possible Causes:**
    - File size too large
    - Unsupported file format
    - Network connectivity issue
    - Storage quota exceeded

    **Solutions:**
    1. Check file size and compress if needed
    2. Verify file format is supported
    3. Test internet connection
    4. Try uploading one file at a time
    5. Convert files to supported formats
    6. Contact administrator about storage quota

    **Error Messages:**
    - "Failed to save changes"
    - "Connection timeout"
    - "Network error"

    **Solutions:**
    1. Check internet connectivity
    2. Refresh authentication session
    3. Copy edited content to clipboard as backup
    4. Try saving again
    5. If persistent, save content externally and contact support
    6. Report error with timestamp and content ID

## Related Topics

    Comprehensive moderation workflows and strategies
    Understanding permissions and role management
    Real-time content moderation for live streams
    Automated content detection and filtering

### [Livestream Moderation](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/moderation/livestream-moderation)

> Enabling safe and engaging live-streaming experiences.

Real-time automated analysis of live video frames & audio to surface or auto-enforce against policy violations with minimal latency. Use this guide to enable, configure thresholds, act on incidents, and continuously tune signal quality.

## Enable the Feature


## How Detection Works

The system continuously samples frames (and audio signals where applicable) and classifies content into risk categories. Each detection produces category confidence scores (0–100%). Your configured thresholds decide whether a stream:

| Outcome | Trigger | System Action | User Experience |
|---------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| Pass | All confidences below Flagged thresholds | Continues | Normal playback |
| Flagged | Any category ≥ Flagged threshold but < Terminated | Marked for review (no auto-stop) | Appears normal to viewers |
| Terminated | Any category ≥ Terminated threshold | Broadcast stopped; post soft‑deleted | Appears as normally ended |

## Detection Categories


## Configuring Confidence Thresholds


## Tuning Playbook


## Acting on Incidents


## Key Metrics

| Metric | Definition | Target / Signal | Action Trigger |
|--------|-----------|-----------------|----------------|
| Flag Volume per Hour | Flagged streams / hour | Baseline after Week 1 | Sustained spike → investigate attack/content trend |
| False Positive Rate (Flagged) | (Flags cleared as OK) / Flags reviewed | < 30% | Higher → raise Flagged threshold |
| Termination Precision | Valid terminations / Total terminations | > 95% | Drop → raise Terminated threshold & audit samples |
| Miss Incidents | Confirmed violations not flagged/terminated | 0 critical misses | Any miss → lower relevant thresholds or escalate model gap |
| Avg Review Time | Time from flag to moderator decision | Within SLA (e.g., < 5 min) | Rising trend → staffing or threshold adjustment |
| Category Distribution | % flags by category | Stable pattern | Sudden skew → targeted abuse or threshold imbalance |

## Best Practices


## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Likely Cause | Resolution |
|-------|--------------|------------|
| Overloaded Flag Queue | Flagged threshold too low / spike event | Raise threshold 5% or add temporary staffing; isolate noisy category |
| Repeated False Terminations | Terminated threshold aggressive or misclassified scenes | Increase threshold; review sample frames; report to support |
| Missed Severe Violation | Threshold too high or model blind spot | Lower thresholds; capture evidence & escalate for model improvement |
| Cost Surge | Increased broadcast minutes + high flag rate | Audit usage, enforce creator guidelines, optimize thresholds |
| Inconsistent Moderator Decisions | Lack of unified rubric | Establish decision matrix & training refresh |
| Slow Review Times | Understaffing or UI overload | Add shift coverage; refine filters; raise thresholds temporarily |


## Next Steps

### [Channel Management](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/chat-management/channel-management)

> Guide for moderators and administrators to search, create, configure, moderate, and analyze chat channels in the Console

<iframe width="720" height="405" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/24XZQHgHZPk?si=-R1n9H5Zq8u8xr-G" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen />

  This page explains how Console moderators and administrators manage channels: discovery, creation, configuration, member governance, lifecycle actions, and activity analytics. Focus: operational workflows (not developer integration).

    Search & filter active, muted, stopped, deleted channels

    Launch Community, Live, or Broadcast channels

    Privacy & notification modes

    Mute, stop, delete, ban, unban members

    Review flagged messages & user chat activity

    Apply governance & engagement best practices

## Access & Filtering

    Go to Chat Management → Channels.

    Use search \+ filter controls at top.

    Identify state (muted, stopped, deleted) via status icons.

    Open target channel for settings & membership.

{/* Removed missing image: Channel List & Filters */}

    - Standard (active)
    - Deleted: Gray trash icon (visible to admins only)
    - Muted: Mute icon (all members read-only)
    - Stopped: Gray stop icon (force-closed; members removed)
    - Show All Channels: Includes active / muted / deleted
    - Hide Deleted Channels: Active & muted only
    - Flagged Messages: Channels containing user-flagged messages for review
    - Audit cleanup: Show All
    - Safety triage: Flagged Messages
    - Operational focus: Hide Deleted

## Channel Actions Overview

    Temporarily suspend new member messages (read-only). Use for cooling-off periods or announcement-only phases.

    Immediately close channel and remove all members. Use for irreversible shutdown (archival or policy breach).

    Hide from non-admin users (soft delete). Still visible to admins with gray trash icon for oversight/audit.

    Default (all subscribed), Silent (suppress all), Subscribe (only opted-in). Adjust to control noise & relevance.

    Public = discoverable; Private = invite-only. Only applies to Community channel type.

## Creating a Channel

    Channels → \+ (Create) button.

    Choose Community, Live, or Broadcast.

    Set Channel ID & Display Name (member-visible).

    Choose Public or Private.

    Pre-mute if staging content before opening.

    Pick Default, Silent, or Subscribe based on expected activity volume.

    Confirm settings and create channel.

{/* Removed missing images: Create Channel Button / Channel Creation Dialog */}

  Use Silent mode for high-volume announcement channels where you want manual opt-in later; use Subscribe mode to reduce irrelevant push volume platform-wide.

## Deleting / Stopping a Channel

    Select target from list.

    Navigate to Settings section.

    Decide: Stop (immediate closure) vs Delete (hide from end users).

    Execute action; verify state icon updates.

{/* Removed missing image: Delete Channel Interface */}

  Stopping a channel forcibly disconnects members. Deleting keeps history accessible to admins but hidden from users.

## Managing Channel Users

    Inside channel, select Members.

    Search or scroll through membership list.

    Mute, Ban, Change Role, or Remove.

    Verify icon/state updates (e.g., ban badge).

{/* Removed missing images: Members List / Member Actions */}

    Temporarily restrict a user from sending messages; reversible at any time.

    Block user and remove them from active participation. Unbanning restores eligibility to rejoin.

    Adjust permissions (e.g., moderator vs standard member) aligned with channel governance.

    Detach user from channel without punitive ban; they may rejoin if channel is public or invited.

    - Ban: User flagged as blocked; cannot rejoin until unbanned.
    - Remove: Membership cleared; no block state.
    - Removing a banned user clears ban status—confirm enforcement intent before removing.

{/* Removed missing image: Banned Indicator */}

  Use ban for policy violations; use remove for inactive or off-topic presence without formal enforcement.

## Chat Activities (Beta)

A user-centric analytics view (Manage Users → Chat Activities) showing per-user metrics:

- Total messages sent
- Channels joined
- Flagged messages involvement
- Channel-level breakdown of participation

{/* Removed missing image: Chat Activities Analytics */}

    Identify high-risk users (flag density), evaluate power contributors, support behavioral audits.

    Prioritize intervention for users generating disproportionate flagged content.

    Spot champions for recognition and retention programs.

  Beta feature: Data refresh latency may apply; cross-verify before enforcement escalation.

## Best Practices

    - Define naming conventions for clarity.
    - Segment high-volume topics into dedicated channels.
    - Use Private Community channels for sensitive collaboration.
    - Default sparingly for high-noise channels.
    - Silent reduces fatigue during heavy announcement cycles.
    - Subscribe mode encourages opt-in relevance.
    - Ban only with documented rationale.
    - Review muted channels regularly; unmute when stability returns.
    - Reassess stopped channels quarterly (archive or permanently remove metadata).
    - Track flagged message ratio per channel.
    - Identify recurring violation patterns (spam clusters, harassment zones).
    - Escalate severe violations per internal protocol.

## Troubleshooting

| Issue                                        | Likely Cause                    | Resolution                               |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Cannot create channel                        | Missing permission / invalid ID | Verify role & unique ID format           |
| Notification mode not saving                 | Unsaved dialog or permission    | Reopen dialog, confirm role rights       |
| User rejoined after remove                   | Remove used instead of ban      | Apply ban for enforcement scenarios      |
| Ban cleared unexpectedly                     | User removed while banned       | Re-ban without removing membership first |
| Channel still visible to admins after delete | Expected soft delete behavior   | No action; working as designed           |
| Messages still incoming after mute           | Delay / client cache            | Confirm mute state; refresh clients      |
| Flagged count not updating                   | Processing delay                | Wait sync cycle or refresh panel         |

## Quick Reference

    Community • Live • Broadcast

    Default • Silent • Subscribe

    Mute • Ban • Role • Remove

    Mute → Stop → Delete (soft)

## Next Steps

    Strengthen enforcement workflows

    Manage users & roles

    Explore chat activity metrics

    Configure official identity

### [Message Management](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/chat-management/messaging-management)

> Guide for moderators and administrators to broadcast, monitor, filter, action, and govern chat messages in the Console

This page explains how Console moderators and administrators manage real‑time chat: broadcasting, monitoring live streams, filtering, enforcing policy, handling mentions, and applying message-level actions.


## Broadcast Messages

Broadcast channels allow only admin / authorized staff to post while all other members are read‑only—ideal for high-signal announcements, policy updates, campaigns.


{/* Removed missing images: Broadcast Channel View / Broadcast Compose Trigger / Broadcast Message Composer */}


## Live Message Monitoring

Admins can enter any eligible channel to observe real‑time messages, apply filters, enforce policies, and send official interventions.


### Pausing Data Stream

If messages scroll too quickly for review, pause to stabilize viewport:


## Message Types

The system supports multiple structured types:

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Text | Plain textual content |
| Image | Message containing an image asset |
| File | Message containing a downloadable file |
| Audio | Message containing an audio clip |
| Custom | Developer-defined payload / structured custom data |


## Filtering


## Mentions Governance


{/* Removed missing image: Mention Settings */}


## Message Actions


## Enforcement Workflow Example


## Best Practices

    - Use Pause only when necessary (avoid missing fast-moving signals).
    - Batch similar actions to reduce context switching.
    - Revisit blocklist quarterly for false positives.
    - Delete only when content violates policy; otherwise educate via reply.
    - Prefer Mute for temporary disruption vs immediate ban.
    - Escalate self-harm / threat content per protocol before deletion.
    - Consolidate multiple small announcements into a single structured broadcast.
    - Avoid marketing repetition; track engagement drop-off metrics.
    - Encourage targeted @user use for clarity.
    - Limit @all to critical updates (target threshold e.g., &lt;2 per week per channel).

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Likely Cause | Resolution |
|-------|--------------|------------|
| Stream not resuming | Pause still active | Click Resume; verify UI state |
| Flag not clearing | Permission or stale state | Refresh; confirm role; retry |
| @all still works after disable | Cached settings | Refresh channel view; re-save setting |
| Blocklist term not applied | Formatting mismatch | Add normalized (lowercase / trimmed) variant |
| Broadcast failed | Network or missing rights | Retry; verify broadcast privilege |
| User keeps reoffending | Mute instead of ban | Apply ban + document rationale |
| Deleted message visible shell | Expected placeholder | No action—design for audit clarity |

### [Communities Management](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/social-management/communities)

> Manage community creation, organization, permissions, membership, and activity in the social.plus Console

<iframe width="720" height="405" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/L08qa3hhQ1k?si=aUStV8KBC_bhTi3y" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen />

  **Goal**: Provide operators and moderators with efficient workflows to create, organize, govern, and moderate user communities at scale.

The Communities section of the Console centralizes everything: creation, categorization, activity oversight, membership governance, and fine‑grained permissions.

    Launch new spaces with avatar, category & visibility
    Review posts, comments, schedules & flags
    Add, remove, ban, or change member roles
    Configure posting, approvals & commenting rules
    Organize & discover related communities
    Surface at‑risk communities & escalation targets

## Primary Workflows

        Navigate: Social Management → Communities.
        Click the \+ (Create New Community) button.
        Avatar, Name, About (≤180 chars), Category.
        Default, Silent or Subscribe (see Notification Modes below).
        Validate visibility & category before saving.
      Screenshot placeholders can be inserted where needed using \<figure\> blocks.
    Use the Activity tab inside a selected community to:

    - View all posts & comments (including soft‑deleted for review)
    - Approve / decline pending (approval-required) posts
    - Schedule future posts
    - Inspect flag counts & reported comment indicators

      Flag filters quickly surface content needing intervention.
    The Members tab provides:

    - Search by user ID or display name
    - Filters: banned / deleted / role-based
    - Role adjustments (member ↔ moderator)
    - Community ban / unban actions

      Banned or deleted users retain status indicators for audit clarity.
    Split across two tabs:

    - Permissions: Posting & commenting governance
    - Settings: Identity & structural attributes (avatar, name, description, category, official flag, visibility)

      Deleting (closing) a community is irreversible. Archive alternatives should be considered first.
    Categories cluster communities by theme to enhance discovery.

    - Create & edit categories centrally
    - Tag multiple communities under a shared interest
    - Enable cross‑promotion (“See more in Fitness”)

## Permissions & Governance

    **Options**:

    - Only admins can post: Restricts creation to moderators/admins (announcement mode)
    - Approve member posts: Member posts enter an approval queue
    - Open posting (default): All members post immediately

      "Only admins can post" and "Approve member posts" are mutually exclusive.
    Global toggle for allowing comments on community stories.

    - Enabled: All active stories accept member comments
    - Disabled: No stories within the community accept comments

      Granular per‑story comment locking is not currently supported.
    Define default push behavior for new community activity:

    - Default: Notifications ON for all members (may opt out)
    - Silent: Notifications OFF & cannot be enabled by members
    - Subscribe: Notifications OFF by default; members may opt in

      Use Silent for low-noise hubs or archive‑style communities.
    Editable fields:

    - Avatar (brand & recognition)
    - Name & Description (≤180 chars recommended)
    - Category (affects discovery grouping)
    - Official Flag (trust / authenticity badge)
    - Visibility: Public vs Private (affects access & join flow)
    - Closure: Permanent removal (non‑recoverable)

## Best Practices

    - Batch resolve low‑risk approvals daily
    - Use categories early to avoid later reclassification overhead
    - Maintain naming conventions (e.g., Region • Topic)
    - Standardize moderator notes for actions
    - Start with open posting \+ approvals for new communities
    - Introduce Only-admin mode for broadcast / compliance spaces
    - Monitor flag ratios: flags per 100 posts as drift indicator
    - Periodically audit moderator role assignments
    - Curate category landing pages with representative communities
    - Highlight official communities for trust signaling
    - Use Subscribe mode for optional-interest clusters
    - Rotate featured categories based on engagement analytics

## Next Steps

    Align moderator capabilities with governance model
    Integrate community workflows with platform moderation

    Track growth & risk indicators across communities
    Manage taxonomy & discoverability structures

### [Stories Management](https://learn.social.plus/analytics-and-moderation/console/social-management/stories)

> Guide for moderators and administrators to create, schedule, view, and moderate Stories in the Console

<iframe width="720" height="405" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/7ZsAIvKEHpY?si=mNRzF-okbcbSPfvG" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen />

  This page explains how Console moderators and administrators manage Stories across communities: creation, scheduling, visibility, moderation, and lifecycle oversight. It focuses on operational workflows (not developer implementation).

    Upload image & video Stories (up to 10 items)

    Plan Story releases up to 30 days ahead

    Track Active, Expired, Scheduled status

    Delete or clear flagged Stories

    Leverage links & narrative sequencing

    Use lifecycle & performance signals

## Accessing Stories

    Go to Admin Console → Stories.

    Search by community name or ID.

    Switch between Active, Expired, and Scheduled.

    Create, schedule, delete, or clear flags as needed.

{/* Removed missing image: Stories Module Entry */}

## Creating Stories

    - Images: up to 1 GB each
    - Videos: under 2 GB, ≤ configured maximum duration (default 90s)
    - Up to 10 media files per Story batch (all publish together as sequential individual Stories)
    - Single creation batch = ordered Story sequence for narrative impact
    Drag to reorder before publishing / scheduling.
    Use sequencing for: event recaps, multi-step tutorials, campaign countdowns.
    Add an optional hyperlink per Story item.
    If URL whitelisting is enabled, destination must be pre-approved or link cannot be saved.
    - Earliest schedule time: ≥ 1 hour from now
    - Latest schedule time: up to 30 days ahead
    - All items in the batch follow the same release time and then display in the order configured
    Console preview approximates in‑app experience (device variances may apply). Validate crop, readability, caption relevance, and link integrity before finalizing.

{/* Removed missing images: Media Upload / Reorder & Links / Scheduling Stories */}

  Reordering and link edits are only possible before publishing or while the batch is still in Scheduled status.

## Story Status Lifecycle

    Currently visible Stories within their active duration window.

    Stories whose visibility period has concluded; retained for reference in Console only.

    Approved Story batches waiting to publish at their scheduled time.

{/* Removed missing image: Status Tabs */}

  The count displayed on a community's Stories card reflects Active \+ Expired only (excludes Scheduled and Deleted).

## Viewing Stories

Within Active & Expired tabs you can:

- View media (image / video)
- Access hyperlinks
- Check comment count (and flagged comment count if applicable)
- View reactions count
- Moderate (delete, clear flags)

{/* Removed missing images: Stories Card in Community / Stories Listing */}

Scheduled tab displays:

- Upcoming batches sorted by scheduled date (latest first)
- Hyperlinks configured per Story item
- Delete option (removes entire scheduled batch)

  Deleted Stories are not viewable in Console once removed. Confirm before deletion.

## Moderation Actions

    Remove inappropriate or outdated Story items. Action is immediate and irreversible.

    After review, clear resolved or invalid reports to maintain accurate escalation focus.

    Delete scheduled batches that are no longer relevant before they publish.

## Operational Best Practices

    - Batch related content for cohesive storytelling.
    - Schedule during high engagement windows (analyze past peak hours).
    - Limit daily volume to avoid fatigue.
    - Maintain consistent aspect ratios & branding.
    - Ensure text overlays remain legible (contrast, safe margins).
    - Keep videos concise and purposeful.
    - Review flagged Stories promptly (ideally within SLA window).
    - Use whitelisted links only; remove any violating destinations.
    - Document deletion rationale for sensitive content categories.
    - Track reactions & comment patterns to refine timing & style.
    - Use links sparingly for high-intent actions (avoid dilution).
    - Sequence multi-part content with clear narrative arcs.

## Troubleshooting

| Issue                      | Likely Cause                      | Resolution                        |
| -------------------------- | --------------------------------- | --------------------------------- |
| Cannot schedule \< 1h      | Minimum lead time rule            | Set time ≥ 1 hour ahead           |
| Schedule \>30 days blocked | Exceeds max window                | Choose date within 30 days        |
| Link rejected              | Not whitelisted                   | Add to whitelist or remove link   |
| Video upload fails         | Size or duration exceeded         | Compress or shorten video         |
| Missing previews           | Temporary network or format issue | Reupload / refresh page           |
| Story not publishing       | Time mismatch / unsaved changes   | Reconfirm schedule & save         |
| Flags not clearing         | Permission or state change        | Verify role permissions & refresh |

## Quick Reference

    10 media files per batch

    Default 90s (configurable)

    1h → 30 days ahead

    Whitelist (if enabled)

## Next Steps

    Configure community settings

    Manage posts & comments

    Strengthen safety workflows

    Evaluate performance trends
